what_it_does: Acceptance test for the edge-kind registry in contracts/node_record: it verifies that Edge.kind defaults to 'data' for old pre-v2 edges, that old Graph JSON still deserializes unchanged, that registered kinds including 'injection' are accepted, that unknown kinds fail loudly at load, and that every registered kind is reflected in contracts/AGENTS.md.
observations: Imports EDGE_KINDS, EDGE_KIND_DEFAULT, SCHEMA_VER, Edge, Graph, and NodeInstance from contracts.node_record.
Defines a global PASS counter and a check(label, cond) helper that asserts, increments, and prints.
Tests an old Edge created without a kind field and checks it defaults to 'data'.
Tests an old Graph JSON string with bare-dict edges deserializes via Graph.model_validate_json.
Tests that kind='injection' validates and that 'data', 'injection', 'gate', and 'fan_in' are in EDGE_KINDS.
Tests that kind='telepathy' raises an exception at load time.
Reads contracts/AGENTS.md and asserts every EDGE_KINDS entry appears as a substring and that 'EDGE_KINDS' is named in the file.
Includes a docstring citing Concurrent Cognition C1.3, R2-FOLD H5, C9.4/H5, and AGENTS.md rule 2.
Provides a direct run command: PYTHONPATH=. python tests/edge_kinds_acceptance.py.
standouts: Drift-home assertion that scans contracts/AGENTS.md for every machine-registered edge kind.
Schema-additive backward-compatibility test using the exact pre-v2 edge shape.
Self-executing acceptance script with inline run instructions rather than a pytest test file.
conventions: Docstring cites design rules and includes a run command.
Uses pydantic model_validate_json for graph deserialization.
Uses assert with custom failure messages.
Breaks typical pytest convention by using a manual check helper and global pass counter instead of pytest test functions or classes.
concerns: NodeInstance is imported but never used.
Global mutable PASS counter is incremented as a side effect of check().
Expected kinds ('data','injection','gate','fan_in') are hardcoded separately from the EDGE_KINDS registry, so the test can become stale.
Drift-home check uses a simple substring match against AGENTS.md, which may false-positive or miss context.
Unknown-kind test catches generic Exception rather than a specific validation error.
No __main__ guard; importing the module would execute the tests.
notes: A self-running acceptance script that guards the edge-kind registry against silent drift and backward-incompatible schema changes.
questions: What is the full set of EDGE_KINDS and where exactly is it defined?
What do the 'gate' and 'fan_in' kinds mean semantically?
How is contracts/AGENTS.md expected to document each kind (section, table, inline mention)?
Why is NodeInstance imported if it is not used?
What is the intended CI integration for this script?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the docstring states this is the drift assertion for the edge-kind registry, and the code tests schema-additive loading, registered kinds, loud failure, and drift-home reflection.
intention_signals: It is trying to enforce that the new edge-kind vocabulary remains documented, remains backward-compatible with old stored graphs, and fails explicitly when misused, treating the registry as a first-class contract.
novelty: A drift-home test that scans a human-readable constitution file (contracts/AGENTS.md) for every machine-registered edge kind, bridging the code registry and design documentation.
contribution: Provides an executable guard for the edge-kind registry: it demonstrates expected default behavior, validates that registered kinds are accepted, ensures unknown kinds raise at load, and checks that the registry stays reflected in the project's agent constitution. A fused system would keep this as a living acceptance contract for the Edge schema and its documentation.
summary_for_embedding: A self-running acceptance test for the edge-kind registry that verifies schema-additive default loading, registered kinds including injection, loud rejection of unknown kinds, and drift-home coverage in contracts/AGENTS.md.
intention_for_embedding: To prevent silent rot in the edge-kind vocabulary by coupling the code registry to its design documentation and preserving backward compatibility with pre-v2 graph files.

symbols.does: Asserts a labeled condition, increments a global pass counter, prints an ok line, or raises AssertionError.
symbols.description: The check helper is a lightweight inline assertion harness used by the acceptance script. It takes a human-readable label and a boolean condition, asserts the condition with the label embedded in the failure message, increments the global PASS counter on success, and prints a formatted ok line. It effectively replaces a pytest assertion for this self-running script.
